steps.title: Find the slope of line l
steps.content: Line $k$ has slope $m$. Perpendicular lines have slopes whose product is $-1$, so line $l$ has slope $-\frac{1}{m}$.

steps.title: Write line l using point-slope form
steps.content:
Line $l$ passes through the intersection point $(2, 8)$ with slope $-\frac{1}{m}$:
$$y - 8 = -\frac{1}{m}(x - 2)$$

steps.title: Evaluate at x = 3 and test each choice
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 3$:
$$y = 8 - \frac{1}{m}(3 - 2) = 8 - \frac{1}{m}$$
So line $l$ passes through $\left(3,\ 8 - \frac{1}{m}\right)$.
  • A. $(3, 8 - \frac{1}{m})$ — exactly the point we derived. Correct.
  • B. $(3, 8 + \frac{1}{m})$ — wrong sign; this would require slope $+\frac{1}{m}$, not perpendicular.
  • C. $(3, 8 - m)$ — uses slope $-m$ (same magnitude as $k$, not the negative reciprocal). Eliminated.
  • D. $(3, 8 + m)$ — uses slope $+m$, which is line $k$ itself, not perpendicular. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
